title: x-security
description: Vendor-neutral OpenAPI extension declaring per-endpoint API security policy. Writ compiles this into Kong, Coraza, BunkerWeb, OpenAppSec, Cloudflare, AWS API Gateway, and host-firewall configurations. v0.8 (deferred-residuals) adds: graphql.operations[] per-operation authz + cost (capKey graphql.operations.authz, API1/API5, OVERRIDE-ONLY everywhere — requires an operator-supplied GraphQL-aware processor; the coarse block limits get the distinct capKey graphql.staticLimits, API4); request.serializeBy + request.concurrencyLimit live-concurrency serialization (API6, PARTIAL at best — edge serialization only, NOT in-handler transaction atomicity); and request.dataAtRest ADVISORY-ONLY at-rest posture declaration (Writ-native SSEC-STORAGE, NOT gateway-enforced — compiles to nothing, drives an out-of-band scan finding). The unreachable residuals token-entropy and session-rotation get ZERO new fields (detect-and-flag / documented gateway non-goal). v0.7 (edge-enforceable-residuals) adds: injectionGuard sinks 'deserialization' (SSEC-INJECTION) and 'ai-prompt' (Writ-native SSEC-PROMPT); top-level audit logging policy (logging, Writ-native SSEC-AUDIT); response.forbidArrayRoot JSON-hijacking defense (API3); request.idempotencyKey replay/double-submit defense (API6); and capability wiring for the already-declared authentication.passwordPolicy / authentication.accountLockout enforcement (API2). v0.6 added: per-arg injection hardening (W19 request.schema.<f>.injectionGuard, attributed to the Writ-native SSEC-INJECTION category). v0.5 added: identity-flexibility (S-10 RuleRef namespaces: principal/session/header), outbound trust (S-11 outboundCalls), auth depth (S-12 mfa/lockout/passwordPolicy/tokenSources), error scrubbing (S-13), composite-ID semantics (S-14 identifier combinator), open-redirect (S-15 redirectAllowedDomains), session fixation (S-16 sessionRotateOnAuth), nonce replay (S-17 nonceCacheTtl), TLS floor (S-18 tls).

profile.description: Optional security profile preset name (R1.17). Per-field overrides allowed.

outboundCalls.description: v0.5 S-11: outbound calls this endpoint may make. Used to enforce signed requests, response schema, and TLS floor.

SsecId.description: Writ-native security category id for classes OWASP-API-2023 does not enumerate (Injection, LLM prompt-injection, audit logging, at-rest storage posture). Kept as a parallel pattern so the pure OWASP cells (^API([1-9]|10):2023$) cannot be overloaded or corrupted. v0.7 adds PROMPT (LLM prompt-injection) and AUDIT (audit logging) alongside INJECTION. v0.8 (deferred-residuals) adds STORAGE — an ADVISORY-ONLY at-rest posture class (encrypted/hashed/tokenized declaration). STORAGE is structurally unreachable by the gateway (it never sees the DB write); it NEVER compiles to enforcement and its capability is hard-pinned override-only/unsupported on every target. It exists only to document declared posture and drive an out-of-band scan finding.

RuleRef.description: Reference to a value resolved at request time. v0.5 S-10 widens namespaces from {jwt,request,resource} to {jwt,principal,session,header,request,resource}. Semantics: jwt.* = claim from a validated JWT (e.g. jwt.sub); principal.* = identity attribute from ANY auth scheme — generator translates per-target (custom-token, opaque-bearer, etc.); session.* = server-side session store / cookie-backed session; header.* = arbitrary request header verbatim (e.g. header.X-User-Id); request.* = request URI / query / body / path params; resource.* = field projected via resourceLookup.expose[].

tokenFormat.description: v0.4 S-1: custom-token wire format. Required when type === 'custom-token'.

validationEndpoint.description: v0.4 S-1: introspection endpoint for opaque custom tokens.

allowedAlgorithms.description: v0.3: JWT signing algorithm allowlist. Asymmetric only — HS* and 'none' are intentionally excluded.

bannedAlgorithms.description: v0.4 S-2: JWT signing algorithm denylist. Accepts the full algorithm space including symmetric HS* and 'none' so deployments can explicitly forbid them. Must not overlap with allowedAlgorithms.

mfaRequired.description: v0.5 S-12: this endpoint must only be reachable when the authenticated principal has completed MFA. Generators translate per-target (claim check, IDP step-up, etc.).

tokenSources.description: v0.5 S-12: which request locations the token may arrive in. Default is 'header'. Listing more than one widens trust surface — explicit is safer.

accountLockout.description: v0.5 S-12: per-identifier credential-stuffing / brute-force lockout policy. identifier is a RuleRef-style key (e.g. 'header:X-Username' or 'request.body.email') used to group attempts.

passwordPolicy.description: v0.5 S-12: password-strength policy for endpoints that accept a password in the request body (login, register, change-password). Generators may translate to a WAF body inspection or pass through to the app for enforcement.

sessionRotateOnAuth.description: v0.5 S-16: rotate the session identifier upon successful authentication. Defense against session-fixation.

allOf.description: v0.4 S-2: allowedAlgorithms and bannedAlgorithms must be disjoint. JSON Schema cannot express set-intersection directly; we enumerate per-algorithm forbidden pairs.

resourceLookup.description: v0.3: resolve a resource by id before evaluating rules. Lets rules compare jwt.sub against resource.ownerId for BOLA defense.

Csrf.description: v0.3: CSRF defense. Pick one method; required fields depend on the method.

identifier.description: Rate-limit key. Single string component, or (v0.4 S-6) an array of >=2 unique components, or (v0.5 S-14) an object { components, combinator } that disambiguates how multiple components combine. The bare-array form is preserved for back-compat and is semantically equivalent to { components: [...], combinator: 'concat' }.

combinator.description: concat: join components into one bucket key (default — matches v0.4 array behavior). distinct: each component gets its own counter, request rejected if ANY exceeds the budget. min-of: bucket key is the lexicographically minimum component value (used for shared-tenant fairness).

unkeyedHeadersStrip.description: v0.3: request headers stripped before cache-key compute. Prevents header-based cache poisoning.

not.description: Wildcard origin + credentials is insecure (R1.9 ac)

IpPolicy.description: R1.13 — allow and deny are mutually exclusive

ParamSchema.description: R1.3 (extended in v0.3 for binary upload hardening)

redirectAllowedDomains.description: v0.5 S-15: open-redirect defense. When this param flows into a redirect target (e.g. Location header, next/redirect URL), only these domain patterns are allowed. Supports literal hosts and glob patterns ('*.example.com'). Only meaningful when type === 'url'.

blockPrivateRanges.description: v0.4 S-4: SSRF defense for ParamSchema with type === 'url'. When true, reject URLs whose host resolves to RFC1918 (10/8, 172.16/12, 192.168/16), link-local (169.254/16, fe80::/10), loopback (127/8, ::1), unique local (fc00::/7), or non-HTTP(S) schemes (file://, gopher://, dict://, ftp://). Use for SSRF defense on user-supplied URLs.

magicByteCheck.description: v0.3: verify declared MIME matches detected magic bytes (file signature).

extensionAllowlist.description: v0.3: lowercase, dot-prefixed file extension allowlist, e.g. ['.png','.jpg'].

denyDoubleExtension.description: v0.3: reject filenames with multiple extensions, e.g. invoice.pdf.exe.

injectionGuard.description: v0.6 W19 (v0.7 adds 'deserialization' + 'ai-prompt'): opt-in per-arg injection hardening. Declares which injection sink(s) this body/query field flows into so the ModSecurity-family targets (coraza, bunkerweb) emit a native operator on ARGS:<field>|ARGS:json.<field> (covers both query/form args and the JSON body): 'sql' → @detectSQLi, 'nosql' → $where/$ne/$gt/$regex operator-token denylist, 'os-command' → shell-metacharacter @rx denylist ([;&|`$(){}<>] / newline), 'xpath' → XPath-metacharacter denylist, 'ldap' → LDAP-special-char denylist, 'code-eval' → eval/exec-marker denylist, 'xss' → @detectXSS (native cross-site-scripting operator), 'deserialization' → unsafe-deserialization preamble denylist (node-serialize _$$ND_FUNC$$_, Java rO0 base64, PHP O:<n>:, python pickle \x80 opcode framing), 'ai-prompt' → LLM prompt-injection heuristic denylist (jailbreak / system-prompt-leak / role-override markers). Per-arg and explicit — replaces the implicit string-type heuristic. 'sql'/'nosql'/'os-command'/'xpath'/'ldap'/'code-eval'/'xss'/'deserialization' are attributed to SSEC-INJECTION; 'ai-prompt' is attributed to the distinct Writ-native SSEC-PROMPT class (one synthetic id per threat class). Never an OWASP-API cell. Edge-enforceable on coraza/bunkerweb only; kong/envoy resolve unsupported.

pii.description: Mark this parameter / response field as PII or otherwise sensitive. Drives the data-exposure (id:428) filter in addition to the built-in sensitive-name heuristic — use for fields whose name doesn't look obviously sensitive (e.g. nationalId, dob).

RequestSignature.description: v0.3: HMAC/Ed25519 signature verification for webhooks.

nonceHeader.description: v0.5 S-17: header carrying the per-request nonce that the signature commits to. Required when nonceCacheTtl is set.

nonceCacheTtl.description: v0.5 S-17: replay-protection TTL. Reject any signed request whose nonce was already seen within this TTL. Requires nonceHeader.

allOf.description: v0.5 S-17: nonceCacheTtl requires nonceHeader (cross-field constraint, since the nonce must be located somewhere to dedupe).

IdempotencyKey.description: v0.7 (API6): replay / double-submit defense for a sensitive business flow. Reuses the nonce-replay dedupe primitive (cf. RequestSignature.nonceHeader/nonceCacheTtl) but keyed on a client-supplied header instead of a signed nonce: a second request carrying the same header value within ttl is rejected (or returns the cached response). HONEST PARTIAL everywhere: stops replay but does NOT provide in-handler race atomicity — two concurrent first-submits can both pass the edge check. Enforced on collection-capable coraza profiles and kong; unsupported on envoy.

header.description: Header carrying the client idempotency key, e.g. 'Idempotency-Key'.

ttl.description: Dedupe window — a repeated key within this TTL is treated as a replay.

SerializeBy.description: v0.8 (deferred-residuals, API6): live-concurrency serialization for a sensitive business flow (double-submit / double-spend funnel). Declares the resource key that concurrent same-key requests must be serialized on. PARTIAL AT BEST, explicitly NOT full — identical posture to IdempotencyKey: the gateway can SERIALIZE same-key requests at the edge (stop concurrent double-submit) but CANNOT make the app's handler transaction atomic — two requests that both pass the edge serialize gate still race inside the handler if the app re-reads stale state. Per-target reality: coraza/bunkerweb stateful collections implement a CRUDE concurrency cap / short-window dedupe on a SecCollection key (PARTIAL — rate-limit-shaped, not a true mutex, best-effort across worker processes); envoy routes to an operator-supplied ext_proc/external limiter (OVERRIDE-ONLY); kong/aws-apigw/cloudflare/firewall/openappsec have no native per-key lock (UNSUPPORTED — no Redis-plugin is emitted). The capability comment MUST state 'edge serialization only — does NOT provide in-handler transaction atomicity'.

key.description: RuleRef-style resource key concurrent same-key requests serialize on, e.g. 'request.body.account_id' or 'jwt.sub'.

scope.description: Serialize globally or per distinct identifier value. Default per-identifier.

DataAtRest.description: v0.8 (deferred-residuals, SSEC-STORAGE): ADVISORY-DECLARATION ONLY. Names body fields (e.g. password, pan, cvv, apiKey) and the asserted at-rest protection posture the app is SUPPOSED to apply. This field is NOT gateway-enforced and compiles to NOTHING enforcing — the gateway structurally never sees the DB write, so it cannot encrypt/hash/tokenize at rest. Its capability is HARD-PINNED to override-only/unsupported on EVERY target (never full, never partial). It exists ONLY to (1) document declared posture as a comment/annotation in the compiled output, and (2) drive an out-of-band Writ SCAN/agent finding under SSEC-STORAGE when the declared posture is absent or contradicted by detected plaintext storage. A finding, not a control.

fields.description: Body field names whose at-rest protection is being declared, e.g. ['password','pan','cvv'].

protection.description: Declared (NOT gateway-enforced) at-rest protection posture for the named fields.

Logging.description: v0.7 (SSEC-AUDIT): declarative audit/access logging policy. The gateway IS the log point, so this compiles natively per target: coraza SecAuditLog/SecAuditLogParts + per-event tagged log actions, bunkerweb modsec audit config, kong http-log/file-log/tcp-log plugins, envoy access_log with a JSON format string. Writ-native (OWASP-API-2023 retired the dedicated logging class, so this is attributed to SSEC-AUDIT, never an API cell).

events.description: Which security-relevant events to log. At least one required.

sink.description: Where the log line is delivered. Defaults to 'stdout' when omitted. 'http-collector' requires sinkRef (the collector URL).

sinkRef.description: Collector endpoint reference (e.g. ${LOG_COLLECTOR_URL}). Required when sink === 'http-collector'.

piiRedaction.description: When true, declared pii:true fields plus the built-in sensitive-name heuristic are masked/omitted from the emitted log line, so the log template never contains the sensitive field value verbatim. Default false for backcompat.

allOf.description: sink:'http-collector' requires a sinkRef to know where to ship the log.

denyUnknownFields.description: v0.3 PW-2: reject request body fields outside `schema`. Default false for backcompat.

allowedFields.description: v0.4 S-8 shorthand: request body must contain ONLY these top-level fields; equivalent to setting denyUnknownFields: true and listing them all in `schema` with the default ParamSchema. Cannot coexist with denyUnknownFields: false.

denyFields.description: v0.8 (API6): denylist of server-controlled fields a client must never set; reject the request if any is present in the body. The mass-assignment defense that does NOT require a full allowlist — use for reserved/privileged keys (objectId, _id, role, isAdmin, __proto__) when the route persists req.body wholesale and the legitimate field set is open.

allowedMethods.description: v0.4 S-3: server-side HTTP method enforcement (separate from CORS preflight).

disableExternalEntities.description: v0.4 S-5: XXE defense. When true, the XML parser must disable external entity resolution and DTD processing. Default false for backcompat; recommended true for any endpoint that parses XML.

disallowXml.description: v0.4 S-5: reject requests with Content-Type application/xml, text/xml, or application/*+xml. Use when the endpoint never legitimately consumes XML.

concurrencyLimit.description: v0.8 (deferred-residuals, API6): max simultaneous in-flight requests for the serializeBy key (concurrencyLimit:1 == strict serialize). PARTIAL AT BEST — edge serialization only, NOT in-handler transaction atomicity. Only meaningful alongside serializeBy.

duplicateParamPolicy.description: v0.3: HTTP Parameter Pollution defense. Default reject.

headerInjectionGuard.description: v0.3: reject header values containing CR/LF/NUL. Default false for backcompat.

pathCanonicalization.description: v0.3: canonicalize URL path (percent-decode, resolve ./..//) before pattern checks. Default false for backcompat.

allOf.description: v0.4 S-8: allowedFields is a shorthand for denyUnknownFields:true; it cannot coexist with an explicit denyUnknownFields:false.

CookieDefaults.description: v0.3: applied to all Set-Cookie headers on this endpoint's response.

ResponseHeaders.description: v0.3: response-side hardening headers. Absent fields are not emitted.

forbidArrayRoot.description: v0.7 (API3): JSON-hijacking defense. When true, reject any response whose top-level JSON body is a bare array (matches ^\s*\[). Compiles to a phase:4 RESPONSE_BODY deny rule on coraza (gated on supportsResponseBodyAccess — partial where regex-over-raw-body) and bunkerweb; kong post-function partial; envoy override-only (ext_proc). Default false for backcompat.

errorScrubbing.description: v0.5 S-13: outbound error sanitization. Strip stack traces / server banners / verbose internal messages from 4xx/5xx responses. statusOverride remaps specific upstream status codes to a generic body (key: '4xx' or '5xx' status code; value: replacement body or empty string).

GraphqlOperation.description: v0.8 (deferred-residuals): per-OPERATION GraphQL policy bound to a single named field/operation (e.g. systemDiagnostics, pastes, importPaste) that the one POST /graphql route funnels. authz reuses the Authorization rule grammar (rule-based jwt.sub==resource.owner for per-resolver BOLA → API1:2023; rbac for per-resolver BFLA → API5:2023). Per-operation maxDepth/maxComplexity/maxAliases override the block-level coarse limits (API4:2023). OVERRIDE-ONLY on every target: a gateway physically cannot evaluate per-resolver authz or per-operation cost without parsing the GraphQL query+variables out of the POST body and resolving the selection set — the SAME GraphQL-aware-processor gap as the response-schema ext_proc. Writ ships SCAFFOLDING (an ext_proc filter + a graphql-operations.json data file and/or a graphql-armor/Apollo-router config artifact); ENFORCEMENT DEPENDS ON the operator supplying the GraphQL-aware processor. Until they do, NOTHING here is enforced — therefore override-only, NOT full, NOT partial. The compiled artifact's enforcement status reads 'override-only' verbatim.

name.description: The GraphQL field/operation name this policy binds to (e.g. 'systemDiagnostics').

authz.description: Per-resolver authorization. Mirrors the top-level Authorization $def grammar (rbac / rule-based / abac). Presence attributes to API1:2023 (per-resolver BOLA) and API5:2023 (per-resolver BFLA). OVERRIDE-ONLY — requires an operator-supplied GraphQL-aware processor; Writ emits only the rule data + scaffolding, never an enforcing filter.

GraphqlPolicy.description: v0.3: GraphQL-specific limits. At least one limit OR an operations[] entry must be set. v0.8 (deferred-residuals) adds operations[] for per-operation authz + cost. The block-level coarse limits (maxDepth/maxComplexity/maxAliases/batchLimit/disableIntrospection/allowedOperations) carry capKey graphql.staticLimits (API4:2023; override-only/partial per target — a target may enforce a crude non-parsing limit). The new operations[].authz carries the distinct capKey graphql.operations.authz (API1/API5; OVERRIDE-ONLY everywhere). The two capKeys are kept separate so the per-operation authz override-only status never contaminates the coarse-limit cell and vice versa.

operations.description: v0.8: per-operation policy bound to named GraphQL fields the single /graphql route funnels. Additive — block-level limits stay coarse; operations[] is OVERRIDE-ONLY (operator-supplied GraphQL processor required). minProperties:1 on the block is satisfied by either the coarse limits or operations[].

WebsocketPolicy.description: v0.3: WebSocket origin, size, and rate enforcement.

OutboundCall.description: v0.5 S-11: declared outbound call this endpoint makes (webhook delivery, downstream service, IDP, etc.). Lets the WAF assert signed-request requirements, response schema, and TLS floor before the call is allowed to leave.

responseSchema.description: OpenAPI 3.0-style schema fragment the response must validate against. Passed through to the generator unchanged.

allOf.description: v0.5 S-11: secretRef is required when the call is signed (signatureAlgorithm !== 'none').

Tls.description: v0.5 S-18: TLS floor for this endpoint. Generators emit per-target equivalents (e.g. ssl_protocols in nginx, MinTLSVersion on a CloudFront distribution).

TargetOverrides.description: R1.15 — escape hatch for vendor-specific config. Values are passed through unchanged.
